lint.command: echo "ESLint not configured yet — lint is a no-op until ci-enhancement feature adds it"

blockingIssues.description: dualvm/test/GovernorLifecycle.ts adds mint coverage by deploying a fresh AccessManager + GovernanceToken pair and manually wiring the mint selector instead of exercising the real governed deployment, while dualvm/lib/deployment/deployGovernedSystem.ts still does not map governanceToken.mint to ROLE_IDS.MINTER. The governed mint path therefore remains unverified and may be unwired in the actual deployment helper.

dualvm/test/LendingHardened.ts replaces the ABI-only reentrancy check with a real DebtPool deposit attack, but it still does not exercise any LendingCore entrypoint. The claimed VAL-OZ-001 coverage for both LendingCore and DebtPool remains incomplete, so a missing nonReentrant guard on LendingCore would still go undetected.

appliedUpdates.description: Added .factory/library/erc4626-debtpool.md documenting that DebtPool relies on OpenZeppelin ERC4626's default _decimalsOffset() == 0 behavior, which keeps the inflation attack net-unprofitable but provides only minimal rounding protection against large donations.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the existing Hardhat TypeScript `as any` workaround in the solidity-worker skill for tests that call non-standard contract methods returned from contract factories.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The governance-scrutiny-fixes review and handoff both noted that new test coverage hit Hardhat contract-factory typing friction and needed `as any` casts to satisfy typecheck, but the current solidity-worker skill guidance does not mention that repo-specific workaround.
